word: G1898 - ἐπεισαγωγή - epeisagōgḗ - ep-ice-ag-o-gay' - Bringing in

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἐπεισαγωγή

- Transliteration: Epeisagoge

- Phonetic: ep-ice-ag-o-gay'

- Definition:

1. a bringing in besides or in addition to what is or has been brought in

a. used of the introduction of a new wife in the place of one repudiated

b. to come in besides or to those who are already within

1. to enter afterwards

c. to come in upon, come upon by entering

1. to enter against

- Origin: from a compound of G1909 and G1521

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Noun Feminine

- Strong's: From a compound of G1909 and G1521; a superintroduction: - bringing in.
